purpose: This policy describes how <<LAB_NAME>> ensures continuity of specimen and result information during shift change, transfer between testing benches, transfer to a reference lab, and return of results to the ordering provider. Hand-off integrity is required by 42 CFR 493.1232 (specimen integrity) and 42 CFR 493.1291 (result reporting).

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Specimen identification and integrity
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: The laboratory must establish and follow written policies and procedures to ensure positive identification and optimum integrity of a patient's specimen from the time of collection or receipt through completion of testing and reporting of results.

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Test report
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: The laboratory must have an adequate manual or electronic system(s) in place to ensure test results and other patient-specific data are accurately and reliably sent from the point of data entry (whether interfaced or entered manually) to final report destination, in a timely manner.

scope: All <<LAB_NAME>> personnel involved in specimen receipt, testing, result release, shift change, and inter-bench or inter-facility transfer.
policy_statements: Every hand-off of a specimen, in-process test, or pending result includes: patient identifiers (name + DOB or MRN), specimen source, test(s), current status, any anomalies (QC issues, instrument flags, repeat needed), and any time-sensitive action.
Shift change hand-offs are documented on the Shift Hand-off log or in the LIS shift report; the outgoing and incoming staff each sign or initial.

Hand-offs to a reference laboratory follow the reference-lab packaging, labeling, and chain-of-custody requirements; the send-out is logged with date/time, courier, and acknowledgment of receipt when available.
Pending critical or time-sensitive results are flagged at hand-off and verbal hand-off is required; receipt acknowledgment is documented.
Hand-off failures (missed information, lost specimens, missed critical-value windows) are logged as non-conformance events and reviewed by the medical director or designee monthly.
procedure_steps: At shift change, the outgoing shift lead reviews open work (in-process specimens, pending results, instrument issues, QC concerns) with the incoming shift lead using the Shift Hand-off log or LIS shift report.
Each open item is verbally hand-off-ed with patient ID, status, and required next action; both leads initial the log.
For bench-to-bench transfers, the originating testing staff member packages the specimen with labeling intact, hand-delivers or logs the transfer, and the receiving staff member confirms receipt.

For reference-lab send-outs, the staff member packages per the reference lab's requirements, logs the send-out on the Send-Out log (patient, test, courier, date/time), and tracks for return-of-result.
Any hand-off failure is logged on the Non-Conformance Event log and reviewed monthly.
definitions: Transfer of responsibility for a specimen, in-process test, or pending result from one staff member, shift, bench, or facility to another.
Documentation of shift change including open work, pending issues, and any required next actions, with sign-off from outgoing and incoming staff.
Audit trail of every specimen sent to a reference lab, including courier, date/time, and return-of-result tracking.
